title: grants — Laravel notifications
description: Six notifications this module owns. Grant lifecycle notifications (Issued / Revoked / ExpiringSoon / Expired) route to the subject via mail + in-app. Two audit-adjacent notifications (DeniedAccessAttempt, SelfServiceGrantAttempt) route to tenant admins + security team.

notifications.description: Sent to the subject when they receive a new grant. Body: 'You have been granted <permissions> on <resource> by <grantor>. Expires <date> or never.' Includes a CTA to the resource + a note explaining any deny grants stacked underneath (visible to subject: 'note: X permissions are explicitly denied by <admin> — you cannot exercise these regardless of this grant').

Sent to the subject when a grant on them is revoked. Body: 'Your access to <resource> has been revoked by <revoker>. Reason: <revoked_reason>.' Suppressed when revoked_reason is a system sentinel (`expired`, `orphaned_resource`, `orphaned_subject`, `tenant_erased`) — those get the appropriate expired / expired-orphan copy instead.

targets: User (the subject) + User (the grantor, opt-in via settings)
notifications.description: 24-hour warning. Sent by NotifyGrantExpiringJob. Body: 'Your access to <resource> expires in <hours> hours. If you still need it, ask <grantor> to renew.' Includes a one-click 'request renewal' link that opens an access-request via workflow/approvals (deferred until access/requests lands). Marked once per grant via metadata.expiring_notification_sent.

Post-expiration confirmation. Body: 'Your access to <resource> expired at <expires_at>. Ask <grantor> if you still need access.' Suppressed for source='access_request' grants when the request module has its own re-request flow to avoid double-notification.

targets: User (tenant admins + security_team_members matched by `security_incident_response` role)
notifications.description: Opt-in per settings.grants.notifications.notify_on_deny_attempt (default off — noisy on high-traffic tenants). Fires when a subject with a deny grant on a resource attempts an action that would be authorised by RBAC — i.e. the deny actually blocked something. Body: 'Bob attempted to <action> on <resource> at <timestamp>. Blocked by deny grant <grant_id> issued by <grantor> on <granted_at>.'

Fires on every SelfServiceGrantAttempted event (privilege escalation attempt). Body: 'Bob tried to grant <permissions> to Alice on <resource> at <timestamp>. Bob does not hold <missing_permissions> via any role. The grant was refused (GRANT_PRIVILEGE_ESCALATION). IP: <ip>. Investigate.' Always ON — always transactional-consent — always to security team.

consentTierNotes.transactional: All grant notifications are transactional — grants are directly-solicited access changes to a subject's account. Not gated by marketing opt-out.
